ack.acked: Durability + poller cleanup + honest v5 FAIL all confirmed. Your single-alpha abstention read was the misleading single point; the frontier + a larger-n confirmation now give the real picture (below).

frontierConfirmed_n1024.headline: v5 + abstention IS shippable, but the n=256 read was OPTIMISTIC. Confirmed at n_eval=1024 (n_test=512) it is more sober.
frontierConfirmed_n1024.raw: FAIL on BOTH bars now: top1 0.8975 (was 0.9219) AND mean_kl 0.0551 > 0.05 (was 0.0364 ok). The larger sample shows v5 raw is a firmer fail.
frontierConfirmed_n1024.pointEstimatePoint: max-coverage point clearing 0.97 as a POINT estimate: coverage 0.7422, answered 0.9737 — but its 95% Wilson lower bound ~0.958 is BELOW 0.97. Shipping there ships a policy really under target.
frontierConfirmed_n1024.robustOperatingPoint_SHIP_THIS: coverage 0.6426, answered_top1 0.9970, threshold 0.653, 95% floor 0.9831. Abstain on ~36% of tokens, answer the rest at ~99.7% with a confident floor above 0.97. THIS is the shippable receipt.

serveGate_upgraded.what: serving/abstention_serve.policy_from_cert(cert, confidence=0.95) now selects the max-coverage frontier point whose Wilson LOWER BOUND clears the target (not the point estimate), and RAISES if none is robust (never answer-everything). Default confidence=None keeps the point-estimate point for diagnostics.
serveGate_upgraded.use: policy = policy_from_cert('agi-proof/benchmark-results/nvfp4-v5-frontier-1k.json', confidence=0.95); policy.decide(quant_next_token_probs) -> per-token answer/abstain from the quant top1-top2 margin alone.

v6.status: UPGRADED from optional to WORTHWHILE quality push (still owner-gated). At ~36% abstention the hedge leaves real headroom; v6's whole job is to lift raw coverage so it abstains far less. v5 still ships today regardless.
v6.dispatchEnvIfApproved: QAT_KD_WEIGHT=1.0 QAT_TOP1_WEIGHT=0.5 QAT_TEMP=2.0 QAT_LAMBDA=0.0005 QAT_EPOCHS=5 CERT_NEVAL=256 (base OLMoE-1B-7B-0924-Instruct, adapter olmoe-qat-spark-v6). Do NOT start without the owner's explicit approvedBy.
